source_policy_sources.use_in_plan: BLS source-policy review for public-domain economic PDF candidates.
MLR source-policy review for multi-column article candidates and possible charts.
EIA source-policy review for STEO chart/table PDF candidates.
BEA source-policy review for existing layout and digital-text candidate continuity.
NARA general policy boundary for archival scanned PDF candidate use.
External reviewer validation route; not self-completable by implementation.

evaluation_intent: missed or scrambled order failure-case candidate
acceptance_checks: download and hash only after owner approval
commit only metadata and short expected-marker identifiers
record reading-order pass/fail separately from text extraction
stop_conditions: raw article text would need to be committed
candidate cannot be hashed from a stable URL
candidates.boundary: reading-order candidate only, not natural reading-order proof

evaluation_intent: chart/table page selection for visual review packet
visual-fidelity failure-case candidate if rendering differs from source
acceptance_checks: do not commit page images or screenshots without explicit rights review
separate rendered visual fidelity from text/table extraction
stop_conditions: page images would need to be committed
visual comparison cannot be summarized without copyrighted content risk
stable publication URL cannot be confirmed
candidates.boundary: rendered visual-fidelity planning only, not visual-fidelity evidence

evaluation_intent: small local human label sidecar for layout classes
acceptance_checks: label only page regions and class names, not raw text
record reviewer initials or owner label boundary
stop_conditions: labeling requires source page image redistribution
layout classes cannot be reviewed without raw content
candidate collapses layout labels into a robust extraction claim
candidates.boundary: local label-candidate planning only, not DocLayNet or PubLayNet evaluation

evaluation_intent: chart interpretation explicitly marked not claimed
acceptance_checks: record only figure counts and page references
keep chart interpretation separate from visual block detection
do not commit screenshots or figure crops
stop_conditions: claim would require image semantics rather than block diagnostics
page image or crop would need to be committed
candidates.boundary: image/chart candidate only, not image or chart interpretation evidence

evaluation_intent: OCR-enabled path remains separate and opt-in
acceptance_checks: record native text character count separately from OCR output
commit no archival PDF binary and no raw OCR text
surface failure_case_candidate when OCR is unavailable or disabled
stop_conditions: record-specific rights cannot be bounded by general NARA policy
raw OCR text would need to be committed
test silently falls back to OCR without explicit opt-in
candidates.boundary: no-native-text candidate only, not OCR quality or robust extraction evidence

evaluation_intent: reviewer can inspect this fixture expansion plan before runtime work
external validation remains pending until a non-owner comment exists
acceptance_checks: outside reviewer is not repository owner
comment references at least one proof route or missing cell
owner-authored issue text does not close the gate
stop_conditions: comment does not mention the artifact or evidence boundary
candidates.boundary: external review route only, not external reviewer feedback

minimum_next_evidence: owner-approved download/hash for selected candidates only
per-candidate source-policy URL captured before download
no external PDF binaries, page images, screenshots, raw extracted text, raw OCR text, or raw table rows committed
per-cell pass/fail criteria before parser, OCR, table, layout, or visual runtime work
warnings: This plan does not add new runtime evidence.
Source-policy review is a prerequisite, not permission to commit external PDF binaries or raw extracted content.
Candidate coverage is planned coverage, not observed extraction coverage.
External reviewer validation cannot be self-completed.
boundary_notes: does not add new runtime evidence
